column1: What is the only required section in a CloudFormation template?
column2: Resources section (without it, CloudFormation has no resources to manage)

column1: What does the Ref intrinsic function return for an AWS Load Balancer resource?
column2: The load balancer ARN (not the DNS name)

column1: How do you get the DNS name of a load balancer in CloudFormation?
column2: Use the GetAtt function to fetch the DNSName attribute

column1: What can cause a CloudFormation creation policy to time out?
column2: Issues preventing the instance from calling cfn-signal and sending a success/failure message to CloudFormation

column1: What can trigger CloudFormation custom resources?
column2: Lambda functions and SNS topics

column1: Can Step Functions be called by CloudFormation custom resources?
column2: No, though they orchestrate workflows with Lambda, they cannot be invoked by custom resources

column1: Can ECS tasks be invoked by CloudFormation custom resources?
column2: No, even though they're suitable for one-time work invocations

column1: What happens if a custom resource function executes but doesn't provide a response to CloudFormation?
column2: The resource times out with an error

column1: How are custom resource function permissions obtained?
column2: Through a function execution role (not the service role invoking the stack update)

column1: What is the AWS::Serverless transform in CloudFormation?
column2: A special transform that converts SAM (Serverless Application Model) syntax into native CloudFormation

column1: What do Ref, GetAtt, and DependsOn contribute to in CloudFormation?
column2: They form the relationship and dependency graph that determines resource creation order

column1: What helper script defines packages, files, and configurations when an EC2 instance is first launched?
column2: cfn-init helper script

column1: What helper script signals back to CloudFormation when a resource creation or update has completed?
column2: cfn-signal helper script

column1: What helper script performs updates on an instance when its parent stack is updated?
column2: cfn-hup helper script

column1: How many signals do wait conditions accept?
column2: Only one signal (they will not track additional signals from the same resource)

column1: What CloudFormation feature allows you to specify a count and timeout for signals?
column2: Creation policies

column1: What CloudFormation feature lets you preview infrastructure changes without executing them?
column2: Change sets (create previews that can be reviewed before execution)

column1: How do you reference resources from nested stacks in CloudFormation?
column2: Use intrinsic functions to access resource properties in the same manner as any other stack resource

column1: What permissions does CloudFormation use when operating on resources?
column2: The same permissions as the user performing the stack operation (unless a service role is explicitly passed)

column1: How does CloudFormation recognize dependencies between resources?
column2: Through intrinsic functions like Ref and GetAtt; arbitrary string references are not recognized

column1: What happens during a replacing update in CloudFormation?
column2: The original resource is deleted and a new resource is created with a new physical ID (CloudFormation creates replacement first, verifies it, then deletes original)

column1: What is the difference between replacing updates and updates with some interruption?
column2: Replacing updates delete and recreate the resource; updates with some interruption have downtime but don't replace the resource

column1: Can you delete a CloudFormation stack if other stacks import its exported values?
column2: No, stacks importing the value must remove the import first before the stack can be deleted

column1: When are old resources that are no longer required removed in a CloudFormation update?
column2: During the cleanup phase after the update completes successfully

column1: What template formats does CloudFormation support?
column2: JSON and YAML only

column1: What are the benefits of CloudFormation over procedural scripting?
column2: Reduced human error through template validation, repeatable and versionable infrastructure, reduced permissions scope when using service roles

column1: What services can act as recipients to custom resource requests?
column2: SNS topics and Lambda functions

column1: How do you access data from a custom resource response in the rest of the template?
column2: Use the Fn::GetAtt intrinsic function

column1: Can on-premises servers receive custom resource requests directly?
column2: No, though they can be part of a custom resource's workflow, they don't receive requests directly
